key_insight: The paper builds new low-regularity finite element elasticity complexes on tetrahedral Alfeld splits, reducing smoothness and polynomial degree while still achieving hybridizable, symmetric stress-conforming elements.
engineering_relevance: This enables more practical and efficient 3D elasticity discretizations for solid mechanics, with no vertex degrees of freedom in the stress space and a path to higher-order hybridizable formulations.
method: Uses local Bernstein-Gelfand-Gelfand arguments on polynomial de Rham complexes over Alfeld splits.
Derives two exact local complexes: an $H^2$-$H^1(\mathrm{inc})$ sequence and a lower-regularity $H^1(\mathrm{curl})$-$H(\mathrm{inc}^+)$ sequence.
Constructs global finite element subcomplexes with commuting interpolation and unisolvent spaces for displacement/incompatibility.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports proved exact sequences, bubble subcomplexes, dimension formulas, and commuting interpolation diagrams, with the lowest-order complex recovering the Johnson-Mercier-Křížek element.
watch_item: The new elements are mathematically rigorous but their implementation may be nontrivial because the construction depends on Alfeld-split tetrahedral meshes and specialized exact-sequence degrees of freedom.
paper_title: Low-regularity finite element elasticity complexes with hybridizable stresses on tetrahedral Alfeld splits
